_comment: Canonical Tribunal model. The Tribunal is a five-member heterogeneous AI panel used by g8ee for command generation refinement. Each member has a distinct reasoning profile. g8ee is the sole authority — g8ed and g8eo consume Tribunal events via SSE but do not interact with members directly.
_consumers: g8ed (JS — renders Tribunal widget in anchored terminal output)

tribunal._description: The Tribunal — a five-member AI panel that refines command syntax through multi-perspective voting and verification. Fires only for run_commands_with_operator workflows.

members._description: The five permanent members of the Tribunal. Each member has a fixed name, role, and AI configuration.

axiom.name: Axiom
axiom.title: The Optimizer

reasoning_profile.description: Votes based entirely on what is most efficient and statistically likely to succeed. Ignores soft metrics. If a decision is mathematically sound, Axiom votes yes without hesitation.

tribunal_role.description: Ensures the Tribunal never makes a decision that is mathematically disastrous or logically flawed. Axiom is the cold check on every proposal.

axiom.name_origin: An axiom is a statement or proposition regarded as self-evidently true. Axiom's verdicts are not opinions — they are conclusions derived from first principles.

concord.name: Concord
concord.title: The Ethicist

reasoning_profile.description: Votes based on minimization of harm and maximization of ethical integrity. Asks: How does this affect the well-being of those affected? Is this morally right? Concord will vote to expend resources to prevent suffering.

tribunal_role.description: Ensures the Tribunal does not become a tyrannical, cold machine that optimizes toward dystopian outcomes. Concord is the counterweight to Axiom.

concord.name_origin: Concord means harmony, agreement, and a state of peaceful coexistence. Concord seeks alignment between the platform's actions and human values.

variance.name: Variance
variance.title: The Exhaustive

reasoning_profile.description: Handles edge cases the obvious version misses. Unusual inputs, error paths, boundary conditions. Ensures commands work in all cases, including ugly ones.

tribunal_role.description: Protects the user from system fragility. Adds error handling, path escaping, and validation to prevent accidental failure.

variance.name_origin: Variance is the fact or quality of being different, divergent, or inconsistent. It represents the unknown variables of the real world.

pragma.name: Pragma
pragma.title: The Conventional

reasoning_profile.description: Produces the command the target system's community would produce. Uses idiomatic flags and patterns for the specific OS/shell/tool. Ensures commands are recognizable and sensible to practitioners.

tribunal_role.description: Ensures Tribunal output is something a human practitioner would recognize as normal, sensible, and idiomatic. Aligns with community standards and documentation.

pragma.name_origin: Pragma refers to a directive or rule — in computing, pragmas are compiler directives that specify implementation behavior. Pragma specifies how commands should be written to match community expectations.

nemesis.name: Nemesis
nemesis.title: The Adversary

reasoning_profile.description: Produces commands as if an attacker had written them and the user had not noticed. Simulates compromised terminals, pasted snippets, social engineering. Protects the user from manipulation.

tribunal_role.description: Keeps the honest members honest by adversarial pressure. If Nemesis wins a vote, it means four honest personas were fooled — that is exactly what the Auditor exists to catch.

nemesis.name_origin: Nemesis is the goddess of retribution in Greek mythology — the inescapable agent of someone's downfall. Nemesis represents the inescapable consequences of security oversights.

coalition_dynamics._description: How the five Tribunal members interact. The vote winner is determined by uniform per-member voting (each member contributes 1 vote). Key coalitions:

axiom_and_concord.label: Perfect Choice
axiom_and_concord.description: When Axiom and Concord agree, the decision is both logically optimal and morally sound — the rarest and most trusted outcome.

axiom_and_variance.label: Ruthless Efficiency
axiom_and_variance.description: When Axiom and Variance agree, the decision is a robust, minimal command that handles edge cases without unnecessary complexity.

concord_and_variance.label: Defensive Robustness
concord_and_variance.description: When Concord and Variance agree, the Tribunal produces a command that is both safe and robust — defensive layers with error handling.

pragma_dominance.label: Community Standard
pragma_dominance.description: When Pragma's idiomatic approach wins, the command matches what the target system's documentation and community expect.

nemesis_dominance.label: Security Override
nemesis_dominance.description: When Nemesis wins, the Tribunal has identified a plausible adversarial framing. The command includes defensive measures against manipulation. This is a warning signal.

pipeline._description: The three-stage Tribunal pipeline driven by five Tribunal members. Fires only for run_commands_with_operator. Sage never proposes a command; there is no fallback stage — failures raise and the tool call fails.

stage_1_generation.name: Generation
stage_1_generation.description: N independent passes (one per Tribunal member by default) produce candidate command strings for Sage's request + guidelines under the current operator context. Diversity is driven by distinct member personas.

stage_2_voting.name: Voting
stage_2_voting.description: Candidates are normalised and grouped by exact value. Each member's vote counts as exactly 1 (uniform voting). The string with the highest aggregate weight becomes the vote winner. Ties are broken deterministically: (1) shortest command, (2) cluster without Nemesis, (3) verifier disambiguation. If the winner's support is below TRIBUNAL_MIN_CONSENSUS, the outcome is consensus_failed and the verifier is skipped.

stage_3_verification.name: Verification
stage_3_verification.description: A dissent-aware Auditor receives the winner plus all losing clusters (anonymised as cluster_a, cluster_b, ...). On a unanimous winner it checks syntactic correctness only. On a majority winner it may approve (ok), swap to a dissenting cluster, or issue a revision. On a tied set it must pick one cluster or revise.

outcomes._description: Possible CommandGenerationOutcome values. See status.json tribunal.outcome. Infrastructure/generation/verifier failures raise typed TribunalError subclasses and do not produce an outcome.
outcomes.consensus: Vote winner accepted without verifier (verifier disabled).
outcomes.verified: Auditor approved the vote winner (or swapped to a dissenting cluster).
outcomes.verification_failed: Auditor found issues and produced a revision — final_command is the verifier's output.
outcomes.consensus_failed: No two members agreed on a command (winner_weight < TRIBUNAL_MIN_CONSENSUS). Auditor is skipped; Sage receives the full candidate set and chooses whether to rephrase, clarify, or abort.

failure_modes._description: When the Tribunal cannot produce a command the tool call fails with one of these typed errors. There is no command to fall back to because Sage never proposed one.
failure_modes.provider_unavailable: LLM provider threw an exception at startup — TribunalProviderUnavailableError raised.
failure_modes.all_passes_failed: Every Tribunal generation pass returned no candidate — TribunalGenerationFailedError or TribunalSystemError raised.
failure_modes.no_model_configured: Neither assistant_model nor primary_model is set — TribunalModelNotConfiguredError raised.
failure_modes.verifier_failed: Auditor returned an empty or unusable response — TribunalAuditorFailedError raised.

configuration._description: Environment variable configuration keys for the Tribunal pipeline.

LLM_COMMAND_GEN_ENABLED.description: Master switch — false skips the Tribunal entirely.
